title: Brogy the Giant
description: On pages 5–7, brogy the giant as luffy and the crew meet Dorry and Brogy, two giant warriors locked in a century-long duel on Little Garden.
scene: Brogy the Giant. Luffy and the crew meet Dorry and Brogy, two giant warriors locked in a century-long duel on Little Garden. The scene focuses on the immediate character conflict rather than background exposition.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Nahahahaha!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Jeez, you should've said so earlier!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: I thought you went and cut all those people up because they didn't cook your favourite food or something!

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Who do you think I am, you!?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: Ahahahaha! Well, whatever. What's done is done!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: I refuse!

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: But you have my gratitude for saving me just now.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: What, why?

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: You're a Princess, Right!?

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: A billion belli should be like nothing for a princess...

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Have you heard of a kingdom called Alabasta?

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: No, can't say that I have.

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: It's a highly civilized country within Grand Line,

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: and it used to be a peaceful one at that too... long ago...

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Long ago?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: The past few years have been mired by revolutionary activities.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Due to radical alabastan revolutionaries, my country is currently in a state of chaos.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: One day, I happened to hear of a certain organization.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: that organ- Zation was "baroque works."

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Apparently, it was baroque works who had been instigating the revoll- tonaries.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: But other than that fact, I didn't know anything else nor could I do anything about Baroque works.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: And so, I made a request to Igaram, who's been watching over me ever since I was a kid...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You mean that guy with the Chikuwa hair?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Even though what I had heard may or may not be just a rumour,

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I asked IGARAM if there were any way I could infilitate Baroque works...

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Because that would be the only way to clear away the dark shadow that looms over Alabasta and determine just what exactly is Baroque work's objective once and for all...

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: Well aren't you a brave princess.

dialogue.speaker: Dorry
dialogue.text: So what exactly is the deal with this "creation of an ideal nation" that Baroque works is apparently scheming towards?

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Wait,

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Don't tell me...

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Grip

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Yes, it's as you might suspect.

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: Although the boss has hinted at a goal of creating an ideal nation,

dialogue.speaker: Brogy
dialogue.text: BAROGUE WORKS' True Objective is to take over the kingdom of Alabasta!

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: And if I don't hurry back to my home country to stop the revolutionaries and tell the people about Baroque works, things will go exactly as they planned!

summary: This part advances the chapter by focusing on brogy the giant. It keeps the main tension clear while preserving the dialogue that drives the scene.
